_fixture_note: ACTUAL output, merged from two live runs (names kept generic). The duplicates/nulls/freshness section is from a real 6000-row table run: duplicates PASSED with exactly 6000/6000 distinct -- disproving the ~113 duplicates suggested by snowflake-schema-explorer's approximate profile (HyperLogLog under-estimation). DATE_OF_BIRTH shows the historical-column exemption (measured, not judged) added after the first version absurdly flagged it stale at 9328 days. range/fk/format/business-rule checks are from a separate real 20000-row order-items table run -- the business_rule_checks entry is the honest cautionary case: 89.88% 'violated' a guessed formula that turned out not to reflect the real (synthetic) data at all, not a real quality problem. Used by fixture-test CI: structural assertions only.

columns.verdict_note: measured only -- historical column (a birth date is supposed to be old)
